subtitle: Lateral shin and outer ankle tendon

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Peroneal issues more common on trail runs and cambered roads

mild.description: Outer lower leg tightness or ache after runs on uneven terrain
mild.can_run_note: Yes — stick to flat even surfaces temporarily
why: Peroneal muscles working harder to stabilise the ankle on uneven ground
Lateral ankle instability from old sprains increasing peroneal demand
Supinated foot strike loading the outer foot and lower leg
relief: Outer lower leg massage with a foam roller or ball
Ice outer lower leg after trail runs
Single-leg balance to improve ankle stability
prevention: Ankle stability exercises: single-leg balance progression
Peroneal strengthening: resisted ankle eversion with band

moderate.description: Outer lower leg pain during runs, snapping sensation around ankle
moderate.can_run_note: Flat road running only — no trail or cambered surfaces
why: Peroneal tendinopathy from repetitive lateral load
Peroneal tendon subluxation — snapping or popping around the outer ankle
Chronic lateral ankle instability increasing peroneal stress
relief: Eccentric peroneal loading: resisted ankle eversion
Ankle brace or strapping for support
prevention: Address ankle instability before increasing trail mileage

severe.description: Outer ankle and lower leg pain, swelling, snapping or giving way
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — peroneal tendon can dislocate or tear
why: Peroneal tendon subluxation or dislocation requiring imaging
Peroneal tendon tear at the superior retinaculum
Chronic lateral ankle instability requiring surgical consideration
relief: Complete rest and physiotherapy assessment urgently
MRI or ultrasound to assess tendon
prevention: Full ankle rehabilitation before return to trail running
Possible surgical repair if conservative management fails

see_physio_if: Snapping or popping around the outer ankle
Swelling on the outer ankle not from a sprain
Outer lower leg pain at rest
